message:
fix(runtime): Add UnifiedInt support to round() builtin

The round(x, ndigits) function was failing with TypeError when ndigits
was a UnifiedInt (the tagged union type for Python integers that can be
either small i64 or big BigInt).

Changes:
- Add handling for UnifiedInt's .small field (i64)
- Add handling for UnifiedInt's .big field (*BigInt)
- Properly bounds-check .small values before casting to i32
- For values outside i32 range, use extreme values (1000 or -1000) to
  trigger the appropriate early return behavior

This fixes round(123.456, 2**31) and similar cases where the ndigits
argument is a large integer represented as UnifiedInt.
